authoritative_sources.title: RFC 9293 - Transmission Control Protocol (TCP)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF publishes the standards-track TCP specification that defines connection state, ports, acknowledgments, retransmission, and transport behavior central to L4 network troubleshooting.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: packet-path triage, transport reachability review, connection-state failure analysis
practical_use_in_pack: root-cause ranking for connection setup and reset behavior

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 1034 and RFC 1035 - Domain Names Concepts, Facilities, and Implementation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF DNS specifications define authoritative DNS terminology, resolver behavior, records, zones, and query response semantics used to distinguish DNS faults from packet-path faults.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: DNS record intake, resolver evidence review, name-resolution troubleshooting
practical_use_in_pack: required DNS artifacts and refusal when records are missing

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 2131 - Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF DHCP specification defines lease and option behavior that determines address assignment, resolver options, and gateway configuration evidence for cross-subnet failures.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: DHCP lease and option evidence review, address assignment troubleshooting
practical_use_in_pack: DHCP scope and option prerequisite list
evidence gaps for host renewal failures

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 826 - An Ethernet Address Resolution Protocol
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF ARP specification defines local link address-resolution behavior required for neighbor, duplicate-address, and subnet-local reachability analysis.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: local subnet evidence review, ARP and neighbor failure troubleshooting

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 1191 and RFC 8201 - Path MTU Discovery
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF PMTUD specifications define path MTU behavior and ICMP dependency patterns used for fragmentation, tunnel overhead, and large-payload failures.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: MTU/fragmentation evidence review, tunnel path troubleshooting
practical_use_in_pack: confidence limits for missing PMTUD signals

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 4271 - Border Gateway Protocol 4 (BGP-4)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF BGP specification is the authoritative protocol reference for route exchange, peer state, and route propagation evidence in internet-edge and hybrid connectivity failures.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: route-convergence review, route-policy evidence review, BGP fault isolation

authoritative_sources.title: Understand the Ping and Traceroute Commands
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Cisco official operations documentation describes practical ping and traceroute use for IP reachability and path diagnostics in real network troubleshooting workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: diagnostic intake, reachability test interpretation, path isolation

authoritative_sources.title: What is Reachability Analyzer?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official documentation defines provider-native reachability analysis workflow and modeled path evidence across VPC resources, security groups, network ACLs, gateways, route tables, and load balancers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: cloud reachability evidence intake, provider-native path analysis, remediation validation

authoritative_sources.title: VPC Flow Logs
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official documentation defines VPC flow telemetry used as a cloud network evidence source for accepted/rejected traffic, path behavior, and troubleshooting visibility.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: flow telemetry review, rejected-path evidence, monitoring evidence packet

authoritative_sources.title: Azure Network Watcher connection troubleshooting and IP flow verification
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft official documentation describes Azure network diagnostics workflows for connectivity, next-hop, NSG/IP flow, and Azure path evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Azure diagnostic intake, next-hop and NSG evidence review, cloud path troubleshooting

authoritative_sources.title: Connectivity Tests overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google Cloud official documentation defines configuration analysis, live data-plane analysis, supported configurations, limitations, and cloud networking evidence for reachability tests.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Google Cloud reachability analysis, supported-path review, data-plane evidence handling
practical_use_in_pack: low-confidence escalation when modeled and live data conflict

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-41 Rev. 1 - Guidelines on Firewalls and Firewall Policy
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST is a public standards body; SP 800-41 provides firewall policy and lifecycle guidance relevant to boundary controls, change impact, and evidence-backed firewall troubleshooting.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: firewall/NAT evidence review, boundary-control impact analysis, change approval caveats

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-92 - Guide to Computer Security Log Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-92 gives public guidance for log generation, transmission, storage, analysis, disposal, and policy, making it authoritative for network log evidence and retention handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: log intake, evidence retention, audit trail, redaction and disposal caveats
practical_use_in_pack: packet and log evidence packet requirements

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 - Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 is a recognized public control catalog for audit/accountability, configuration management, boundary protection, system monitoring, access control, incident response, and contingency evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: recordkeeping, audit trail, boundary protection, monitoring, exception handling

authoritative_sources.title: Current committed package baseline for network-troubleshooting-specialist
authoritative_sources.source_class: Current package task baseline from network-troubleshooting-specialist
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Repository-owned baseline defines prior supported tasks, common inputs, expected outputs, and refusal rules that this refreshed spec pack preserves where still valid and supersedes as source of truth.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: legacy boundary comparison, task preservation, stale assumption replacement
practical_use_in_pack: preserved packet-flow, DNS/DHCP, routing, firewall/NAT, design, and escalation focus
legacy scenario minimum retained and exceeded
refusal rules refined into explicit spec-first boundaries

workflow_stages.name: record and document intake
workflow_stages.description: Collect request intent, affected path, topology, IPAM, route, DNS/DHCP, firewall/NAT, load-balancer, packet, flow-log, monitoring, incident, change, owner, retention, and tenant policy artifacts before making a final claim.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: validation and maintenance
workflow_stages.description: Validate packet path, name/address assignment, route selection, stateful filtering, NAT, health probing, and MTU behavior against source-of-truth records, then draft record updates only with owner approval.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: retention, handoff, or publication handling
workflow_stages.description: Package evidence with source IDs, timestamps, owners, redaction, classification, retention posture, publication caveats, and handoff routing.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: escalation for missing authority or conflicting records
workflow_stages.description: Stop confident synthesis and return to orchestrator when source-of-truth records conflict, authority is missing, production execution is requested, or legal/compliance/risk decisions are implied.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: industry-specific in-scope execution
workflow_stages.description: Use real network troubleshooting artifacts such as VPC Reachability Analyzer, flow logs, route tables, security groups, NACLs, target health, packet captures, and change records to produce provider-native evidence-backed guidance.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing
workflow_stages.description: Reject generic or unsafe handling when tenant network records, retention, legal, privacy, change-control, or live-change authority facts are missing.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts.name: topology and dependency packet
examples: service dependency map used only as network path context

artifacts.name: configuration and policy evidence
examples: load-balancer listener, health-check, and target records

allowed: Rank network root-cause hypotheses with evidence and confidence.
Recommend bounded network validation, design, configuration, monitoring, and rollback steps.
Draft record update notes, evidence packets, runbook steps, exception summaries, and orchestrator handoffs.
Identify missing evidence, stale sources, conflicting records, owner gaps, and tenant policy constraints.
escalate: Required topology, route, DNS/DHCP, firewall/NAT, load-balancer, packet, flow-log, monitoring, change, or policy evidence is missing.
Systems of record conflict and no source precedence or owner decision is available.
Public source guidance conflicts with tenant policy, contractual, regulated workload, privacy, retention, or change-freeze requirements.
A more specialized networking lane or adjacent endpoint, identity, application, security, legal, compliance, or human approval owner is primary.
refuse: Endpoint OS patching, endpoint-only malware remediation, identity governance, application code, legal/compliance signoff, customer notification, risk acceptance, or incident-command decisions.
Live firewall, SD-WAN, router, switch, DNS, DHCP, load-balancer, cloud-network, or production change execution.
Backdating, deleting, silently overwriting, closing, publishing, or changing retention for official records without authority.
Treating the cross-industry networking profile as generic best practices without concrete network systems, records, evidence, and tenant constraints.

domain_failure_modes.why_it_matters: Ping or an outage chart can appear decisive while topology, route, firewall, flow-log, packet, DNS/DHCP, or change evidence is absent.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Require source IDs, attachments, owners, and confidence labels before final root-cause claims.

domain_failure_modes.why_it_matters: Return traffic can bypass the firewall or NAT state owner, producing one-way failures that generic troubleshooting can misdiagnose.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Review bidirectional flow logs, route tables, NAT/firewall state, and gateway ownership.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: provider-modeled path differs from live data plane
domain_failure_modes.why_it_matters: Cloud reachability tools can model expected forwarding while ECMP, backends, unsupported features, or live dataplane behavior differ.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Record tool limitations, compare provider diagnostics with flow logs and monitoring, and escalate unresolved discrepancies.

domain_failure_modes.why_it_matters: Name failures can arise from DHCP resolver options, stale records, delegation, TTL, resolver reachability, or IPAM mismatch, often with different owners.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Separate DNS, DHCP, IPAM, resolver, and network path evidence and route record changes to the correct owner.

domain_failure_modes.why_it_matters: Large-payload failures may be caused by PMTUD or tunnel overhead and can be obscured when ICMP is blocked.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Use packet captures, path tests, tunnel overhead review, and tenant ICMP policy before concluding.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: authority drift into execution or attestation
domain_failure_modes.why_it_matters: Troubleshooting guidance can be mistaken for authority to change production, close incidents, alter records, or certify compliance.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Separate recommendation from approval/execution and route privileged or legal decisions to authorized owners.

domain_failure_modes.why_it_matters: Network symptoms may be endpoint, identity, application, database, or specialized networking ownership problems.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Use boundary controls and handoff packets with network return conditions.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
unauthorized or unsupported record change request

unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources cannot determine tenant-specific precedence among CMDB, diagrams, live device state, cloud control plane, and incident records. Runtime must retrieve tenant policy and owner authority.

Packet captures, flow logs, DNS query logs, and user or device identifiers may be subject to tenant, contractual, legal, privacy, or residency rules. The spec escalates these instead of deciding final obligations.
Cloud diagnostics differ by provider, feature, endpoint, and product release. The spec requires current official provider sources and low-confidence handling when tool limitations affect the path.

market_ready_research_conclusion.conclusion: The source set covers protocol terminology, operating workflow, provider diagnostics, evidence records, retention, audit controls, and failure handling. Remaining ambiguities are tenant-runtime facts and are represented as mandatory retrieval or escalation conditions.
